purpose: Defines the LIGHTWEIGHT swarm command `!p {prompt}` — deep planning and high-quality worker prompts with minimal dashboard overhead. Master writes initialization.json once (plan snapshot) and logs.json entries at init/dispatch/completion. Workers do NOT write progress files, do NOT receive tracker_root/dashboardId/progress paths, do NOT read tracker_worker_instructions.md. Workers return structured results via Task tool only. No master_state.json, no metrics.json. Use for <3 tasks, single wave. Master should RECOMMEND escalation to !p_track when 3+ agents or >1 wave detected (escalation gate prompt shown to user).
gotchas: Workers in !p mode do NOT read tracker_worker_instructions.md and do NOT write progress files — fundamentally different from !p_track
Worker prompts use a DIFFERENT template (TEMPLATE_VERSION: p_worker_v1) than !p_track (TEMPLATE_VERSION: p_track_v2)
If plan results in 3+ agents or >1 wave, master SHOULD recommend escalation to !p_track but the user may explicitly choose !p — proceed if they confirm
initialization.json plan snapshot is ALWAYS written even in lightweight mode (it's the dashboard plan visualization)
If dashboard has previous data, master MUST ASK user before archiving and clearing — never proceed without approval
Pre-assigned dashboard from system prompt is authoritative — master has NO access to any other dashboard
Circuit breaker still applies in !p mode (3+ failures in same wave or >50% remaining blocked)
Master must STAY IN MASTER MODE until all tasks complete — do not exit to serial mode mid-swarm
No master_state.json or metrics.json is written in !p mode
patterns: Two phases: Phase 1 Context+Planning (Steps 1-6) → Phase 2 Execution (Steps 7-12)

Worker prompt template (Step 8): What To Do → Context → Conventions → Reference Code → Files (READ/MODIFY/CREATE) → Dependencies → Sibling Tasks (optional) → Critical Details → Success Criteria → Rules → return format (STATUS/SUMMARY/FILES CHANGED/EXPORTS/WARNINGS/ERRORS)
Decomposition principles: no file overlaps within a wave, minimize dependencies, self-contained tasks, right-sized (1-5 min)
Dashboard updates: write initialization.json snapshot before dispatch, log dispatch/completion/failure events during execution, write final summary log entry on completion
Escalation gate prompt (verbatim shown to user): 'This swarm has {N} agents across {W} waves. For full live tracking, consider using !p_track instead. Proceed with lightweight !p mode?'
Post-swarm verification (Step 12) is conditional: modified existing code in multiple files OR any deviations → dispatch verification agent; purely additive OR all-success-no-warnings → optional
conventions: When-to-use comparison block contrasting !p vs !p_track on speed vs visualization
Escalation recommendation callout with verbatim user prompt
Dashboard Updates section enumerating every write point
What Workers Do NOT Do bulleted list explicitly contrasting !p with !p_track worker behavior
